action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 12's residual adapter degraded val PESQ from 2.85 to 2.22, indicating the adapter was not truly initialized to identity (likely due to BatchNorm/bias shifting the signal). Full fine-tuning in iter 11 also destroyed the pretrained weights. The pretrained MetricGAN+ still holds our best test score of 3.134. Analysis: To safely improve the baseline, we need (1) a tiny adapter with no normalization layers and strict zero-initialization so output=input at start, (2) a perceptual loss (MR-STFT+L1) that correlates with PESQ better than raw waveform L1, (3) batched validation to avoid the crippling ~87 min file-by-file validation we saw previously. Decision: Rewrite model.py/train.py/predict.py with these fixes. Run a 10-epoch training with lr=1e-4, batched validation on 100 files, and patience=3 early stopping. If val PESQ beats the pretrained baseline, we'll submit the adapter checkpoint.
